Home → Discography → Evolution of Elevation

Evolution of Elevation

2006 · Mob Life Records

Released in 2006 on Mob Life Records, initially as an internet release before receiving a physical CD pressing. Produced entirely by Hella Tight, the album runs 13 tracks and features guests from Bizzy's 7th Sign circle. Lyrically it sits at the crossroads of street rap and syncretic spirituality, blending Christian and Islamic references with autobiographical reflection. Bizzy Bone has later described himself as a follower of Bay’ah, which he characterizes as a blend of different belief systems and religions. One of four solo albums Bizzy released in 2006, it represents the original, unaltered version of a body of work later repackaged multiple times.

"Hey Miss computer hacker, hey Mrs. Slacker in school ah, You´re meeting your teacher, I'm slappin you with my ruler."

— To the Top
01 Intro produced by Hella Tight —
02 You Don't Really Wanna Be Me produced by Hella Tight feat. Aeileon El Niño —
03 Streets on Fire produced by Hella Tight feat. 7th Sign Regime —
04 Bye Bye produced by Hella Tight —
05 To the Top produced by Hella Tight —
06 Know All About You produced by Hella Tight —
07 Memories produced by Hella Tight —
08 My Name Is Bryon (Interlude) produced by Hella Tight —
09 Evolution of Elevation produced by Hella Tight —
10 The Truth produced by Hella Tight —
11 Bye Bye (Remix) produced by Hella Tight —
12 Still Know All About You produced by Hella Tight —
13 Outro produced by Hella Tight —
